questions.question: Define binary tree traversal. List the four main types of binary tree traversals with their order of node visitation.
keyPoints: Traversal is systematic visit of all nodes in a tree
Pre-order: Node → Left → Right (NLR)
In-order: Left → Node → Right (LNR)
Post-order: Left → Right → Node (LRN)
Level-order: Level by level, left to right

questions.question: Write pseudo-code for recursive In-order traversal of a binary tree.
keyPoints: Function takes root node as parameter
Check if root is NULL, return if so
Recursively traverse left subtree
Visit/print root node
Recursively traverse right subtree

questions.question: Explain why Post-order traversal is used for deleting a binary tree. Also state its time and space complexity.
keyPoints: Post-order visits children before parent
Must free child nodes before parent to avoid memory leaks
Time complexity: O(n) - visits each node once
Space complexity: O(h) - recursion stack, h=height

questions.question: What is the difference between Depth-First Search (DFS) and Breadth-First Search (BFS) in tree traversal?
keyPoints: DFS explores as deep as possible before backtracking - uses stack
BFS explores level by level - uses queue
Pre/in/post-order are types of DFS
Level-order is BFS
DFS uses less memory in wide trees, BFS in deep trees

questions.question: State and explain the application of In-order traversal in Binary Search Trees.
keyPoints: Inorder of BST gives sorted/ordered sequence
Left subtree has smaller values, right has larger
Inorder produces ascending order
Used for: sorted output, finding kth smallest, validation

questions.question: Write pseudo-code for iterative Level-order traversal using a queue.
keyPoints: Initialize queue with root
While queue not empty
Dequeue node, visit it
If left child exists, enqueue
If right child exists, enqueue
Continue until all levels processed

questions.question: Define Morris Inorder Traversal. What is its advantage over recursive traversal?
keyPoints: Morris traversal uses no extra space (O(1))
Creates temporary threaded links to ancestors
Modifies tree temporarily to find paths back
After traversal, tree is restored
Advantage: Constant space vs O(h) space

questions.question:
For the binary tree below, write the sequence of nodes for all four traversals:
      15
     /  \
   10    25
  /  \   /
 5   12 20  30
keyPoints: Show step-by-step for each traversal

questions.question: Write recursive and iterative algorithms for Pre-order traversal. Analyze their time and space complexities.
keyPoints: Recursive: NLR - visit root, recurse left, recurse right
Iterative: Use stack, push right first then left
Both O(n) time - visit each node once
Recursive space: O(h) - call stack
Iterative space: O(h) - explicit stack
Worst case skewed tree: O(n) space, balanced: O(log n)

questions.question: Construct the expression tree for (a+b)*(c-d) and show its Pre-order, In-order, and Post-order traversals. Explain how each traversal represents an expression form.
keyPoints: Tree: * at root, + at left, - at right, leaves a,b,c,d
Pre-order (prefix): *+ab-cd (Prefix notation)
In-order (infix): a+b*c-d (needs parentheses: (a+b)*(c-d))
Post-order (postfix): ab+cd- * (Postfix notation)
Pre/post-order don't need parentheses for evaluation

questions.question: Explain the concept of Threaded Binary Tree. How does it allow inorder traversal without recursion or stack? Discuss its types.
keyPoints: Threaded tree replaces null pointers with threads
Left thread points to inorder predecessor
Right thread points to inorder successor
Single thread vs double thread variants
Allows O(1) space traversal
Must distinguish between threads and actual child pointers
